title: ageratina cuatrecasasii
intro: Ageratina cuatrecasasii, also known as Cuatrecasas' ageratina, is a plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to Mexico and Central America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to be effective in treating wounds, fever, and other ailments. The health benefits of Ageratina cuatrecasasii include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and act as an antioxidant, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Ageratina cuatrecasasii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
